meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no design skills (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Template to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with UI. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to build professional, design-ready web apps as a beginner developer.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template eliminates design paralysis by providing pre-styled, industry-standard components. By pairing this with Claude Code, you can automate the boilerplate and focus on business logic. This workflow transforms your lack of design experience into a competitive advantage, allowing you to ship production-ready applications in record time.
content.intro:
You have the vision and the logic, but the empty canvas stares back with the cold indifference of a blank CSS file. For most student developers, the 'design wall' is where great ideas go to die, buried under the weight of alignment issues and color palette theory. You didn't come here to be a graphic designer; you came here to build the future.

That stops today. The Antigravity template isn't just a starter kit—it’s a battle-tested architecture designed to make your code look like it was built by a senior agency team. When you combine this with the raw power of Claude Code, you stop worrying about pixels and start shipping impact. Let's build.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Environment
steps.description: Set up the project foundation using the template's pre-configured design system which handles typography and spacing automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard template with Tailwind CSS and Shadcn UI integration.

steps.title: Map Your Functional Requirements
steps.description: Define the app's core features clearly so the AI understands the business logic before it touches the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feature breakdown for a task management app with user authentication and real-time database syncing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my requirements.md and create a task list for the backend implementation.'

steps.title: Generate UI Components with Context
steps.description: Use the template's design language to build out your interface components without writing custom CSS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard component using Antigravity layout patterns and existing Shadcn UI tokens.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the dashboard layout using the Antigravity component library structure.'

steps.title: Wire Business Logic to UI
steps.description: Connect your backend services to the front-end components while maintaining the template's strict type safety.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Connect the authentication state to the Navbar component and ensure all types are strictly defined.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate Supabase auth with the generated UI components and fix any type errors.'

steps.title: Automated Quality Assurance
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scan for common UI pitfalls and ensure the design remains consistent with the template standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current codebase for design consistency and accessibility compliance against WCAG standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Perform a full code and UI accessibility audit on the current directory.'

pro_tips: Always rely on the template's design tokens instead of hardcoding hex values to ensure perfect visual harmony.
Use Claude Code's 'edit' mode to refactor existing components rather than regenerating them to maintain your custom state.
Prioritize 'Mobile-First' responsiveness by testing the layout in the browser console at 375px width during development.
Keep your component files small; if a component exceeds 150 lines, it is time to break it into smaller sub-components.
Leverage the Antigravity 'preview' mode to catch visual bugs before committing your code to the repository.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is running, deploy to Vercel using the built-in CI/CD pipeline, collect feedback from peers, and iterate on one feature per week.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the architecture and the AI-assisted tools to bypass the design learning curve and focus on building high-impact software. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' design and start shipping the 'perfect' functionality. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
